snippet.title: Can Chris Bowden Ace His General License Test Tonight? 📻
snippet.description:
The excitement is building on HamShackTV Live as the crew gears up for a massive night of amateur radio action. Kicking things off, Nancy from the Raleigh Amateur Radio Society stops by to share an exclusive preview of the upcoming RARSfest, breaking down everything attendees need to know about the Southeast’s premier radio event, from must-see vendors to expert advice for newcomers. But the real tension lies in the second hour, where Chris Bowden steps into the spotlight to tackle his General license exam. It is a high-stakes session that blends community updates with the thrill of live testing, making it a must-watch for anyone passionate about the hobby. See how the preparation pays off as the team aims for a major milestone.

snippet.title: Build Your Own Radio (Even as a Beginner) – Homebrew Ham Radio with ZL2BNE
snippet.description:
Neville Marr, ZL2BNE, is a New Zealand amateur radio operator known for turning homebrew radio into something approachable and practical. He’s designed and shared projects like a 40-meter SDR receiver kit and regularly operates using self-built SDR transceivers, including the T41 platform. Along the way, he’s supported wider ham radio initiatives such as Reverse Beacon Network stations, and today he helps inspire others to get started building and experimenting with their own gear.

Join us as we explore how you can get involved in portable radio, QRP, and more in this episode of the All Portable Discussion Zone (AP/DZ). Every aspect of portable operations is covered in this biweekly podcast, from news and gear to achievements, the workbench, contests, awards, and beyond.

snippet.title: Montana's Tag Allocation Wins the "Gold Medal" for Complexity
snippet.description:
Montana takes the "gold medal" for the most complicated tag allocation in the West. From bonus points vs. preference points to the multi-step general license pool, even seasoned hunters like Randy Newberg find the math exhausting.

In this Short, we break down why the system is so convoluted and discuss a potential "simple" fix: making nonresidents choose between a General License or a Limited Entry tag. Is it time to ditch the "haircut" on refunds and simplify the odds?

snippet.title: ¿VOTAREMOS LOS VENEZOLANOS EN EL EXTERIOR? Cómo la Licencia 53 de Scott Bessent Cambia el Tablero
snippet.description:
Licencia General 53 del Departamento del Tesoro de los EEUU del 24 de marzo de 2026 LO CAMBIA TODO.
¿Se les acabó el cuento del bloqueo para no dejarte votar? ¡Atención! El Departamento del Tesoro de los Estados Unidos, bajo la dirección de Scott Bessent, acaba de publicar un documento que cambia el tablero logístico para todos los venezolanos en el mundo.

La Licencia General número 53, firmada oficialmente por Bradley T. Smith, director de la OFAC, autoriza las transacciones financieras necesarias para que las misiones diplomáticas de nuestro país puedan operar legalmente en suelo estadounidense. Se acabó el pretexto de que "el banco no nos deja trabajar".

En esta entrega de "El Profe Ideas Políticas", educamos con la verdad y rigor técnico:

¿Qué es la Licencia 53?: No es para sacar la cédula de identidad en el extranjero (ese trámite sigue siendo presencial en Venezuela). Esta medida es para que los consulados tengan cuentas bancarias y paguen alquileres, servicios y personal.

El impacto en el voto: Sin una sede física operativa, el CNE no tiene dónde instalar las máquinas para el Registro Electoral en el exterior.

El fin de las excusas: Durante años culparon a las sanciones, pero con la firma de Bradley T. Smith ese argumento ha muerto oficialmente. Si el registro no se abre ahora, es por pura falta de voluntad política en Caracas y no por impedimentos de Washington.

La transición de nuestro país requiere ciudadanos informados que no se dejen engañar por retóricas vacías. ¡Suscríbete, activa la campana y comparte este video para que la verdad llegue a más personas!

#Licencia53 #VotoEnElExterior #Venezuela2026 #MariaCorina #ElProfeIdeasPoliticas #OFAC #Transicion #SinExcusas #ScottBessent #BradleyTSmith #VotoVenezolano

snippet.title: The Antenna That Connected Our Worlds
snippet.description:
The Antenna That Connected Our Worlds

A tall, silver ham radio antenna in the backyard of a modest bungalow serves as a beacon of connection for a grandfather who spent his life speaking to the world. As the years pass, the tower stands witness to his aging, the addition of a wheelchair ramp, and the eventual silence of the airwaves, culminating in the complete erasure of the home, leaving only the concrete foundation of the signal that once brought the world to his doorstep.

2008 → 2011 → 2014 → 2017 → 2020 → 2023 → 2025 → 2026

#shorts #googlemaps #streetview #nostalgia #timelapse #beforeandafter #timepassage #yearsapart #memories #googleearth

snippet.title: Typical Parks on the Air Morse Code Exchange - contact with W8TEC, WV #HamRadio
snippet.description:
This is what I consider a typical Parks on the Air QSO.  

W8TEC and K4OCY are ham radio call signs.  

TU - thank you
UR - you are or your
55N - 559 - RST signal report
BK - break
72/73 - best regards 
de - this is 
dit dit - cya

Setup:

Yaesu FT-818 Radio

End-fed half-wave antenna ~25 ft high
Horizontal wire, broadside east-west
10Ah battery

Custom power box with solar charging capability

Direct RF communication using HF.

snippet.title: Is The SARK100 Antenna Analyzer Good Enough For The Average Parks On The Air Activator?
snippet.description:
The Rigexpert is undeniably the gold standard of antenna analyzers, but at over $600 CAD, is it absolute overkill for the average Parks on the Air (POTA) activator? Today, we’re looking at the $70 SARK100 from Amazon to see if this budget-friendly analyzer gives you the basic intelligence you actually need in the field.

When you're out in the park throwing an End-Fed Half Wave into a tree, you really only need to know a couple of things: Is my antenna resonant, what is my SWR, and am I going to fry my radio? While the Rigexpert offers incredible build quality, beautiful colour sweeps, and pinpoint accuracy, the SARK100 provides the basic SWR, impedance, capacitance, and inductance readings at a fraction of the cost.

In this video, I'll give a basic introduction to the $70 SARK100. We’ll look at its core features, ease of use, and field portability, and discuss whether this cheap Amazon alternative is a smart buy for your portable ham radio kit or just a waste of money.

👇 Let me know in the comments: What antenna analyzer do you keep in your POTA bag? Are you Team Rigexpert, Team SARK100/NanoVNA, or do you rely on your radio's internal SWR meter?

snippet.title: 2026 HVAC Certification Exam Prep: Full Length Practice Test For Your License
snippet.description:
2026 HVAC Certification Exam Prep: Full Length Practice Test For Your License

Starting your career as an HVAC technician in 2026 requires passing the certification exam with absolute confidence. Many students struggle with the complex technical questions found on the EPA 608, NATE, and journeyman exams. This video provides a comprehensive practice test designed to mirror the actual questions you will face this year.

Every question in this session includes a detailed breakdown of the logic behind the correct answer. Preparing for your HVAC license becomes much simpler when you have access to the right study resources. This test covers core principles, refrigerant handling, safety protocols, and advanced system diagnostics relevant to current 2026 industry standards.

Passing the test is the first step toward a lucrative career in the trades. Statistics show that technicians who use practice exams have a significantly higher success rate than those who rely solely on textbooks. This session is designed to sharpen your knowledge and identify specific areas where you need more study time.

Beyond these free training videos, we provide comprehensive study guides, hands-on workshops, and personalized career coaching to ensure you succeed in the HVAC industry. Our resources are updated monthly to reflect the latest changes in mechanical codes and environmental regulations.

Securing your 2026 HVAC certification opens doors to specialized roles in commercial refrigeration, residential heating, and green energy solutions. Use this practice test as a diagnostic tool. Pause the video for each question and see if you can find the answer before the reveal.

Consistency is the key to mastering these concepts. Reviewing these materials daily will build the muscle memory needed for the actual testing environment. Share this video with your fellow students or coworkers who are also preparing for their certification this year. Saving this video to your study playlist allows for quick access during your commute or breaks.

Your future as a certified HVAC professional starts with the work you put in today. Let us get into the first set of questions and see where your knowledge stands for the 2026 exam cycle.

snippet.title: The Beginner's Guide to Spotting on the POTA Website
snippet.description:
Are you new to Parks on the Air (POTA) and wondering how to get more "hunters" to find your signal? The secret is learning how to "spot" yourself!

In this beginner's guide, we break down exactly how to use the Parks on the Air website to let the amateur radio world know you are on the air. Whether you're activating a beautiful Provincial Park here in Canada or a National Park down south, spotting is the best way to fill up your logbook fast.

We’ll cover what a spot is and how to navigate the POTA.app page from your smartphone or tablet, and exactly what information you need to type in. No confusing jargon—just a simple, step-by-step walkthrough for new hams!

🔗 HELPFUL LINKS MENTIONED IN THIS VIDEO:
• Official Parks on the Air Website: https://pota.app
• Find Canadian POTA Parks near you: https://pota.app/#/map
• Radio Amateurs of Canada (RAC): https://www.rac.ca

🍁 ABOUT THIS CHANNEL:
My channel is dedicated to helping Canadians (and hams worldwide!) learn the ropes of amateur radio. From getting your Basic license to making your first HF contact, we are here to help you get on the airwaves.

✅ DON'T FORGET TO SUBSCRIBE!
If you found this video helpful, please hit the LIKE button, subscribe to the channel, and leave a comment down below telling me which park you plan to activate first!

73 and happy activating!

#HamRadio #AmateurRadio #POTA #ParksOnTheAir #HamRadioCanada #HamRadioBeginner #QRP #PortableRadio #HamRadioEducation #VE9 #portableradio

snippet.title: My Park Was Invaded By Ham Radio Nerds!
snippet.description:
And that's not the worst part.  Out of all the places one can operate at Huntsville State Park, they had to pick the one spot I use.  How dare them!  But they have a cool group called The Houston POTA Group.  They get together several times throughout the year to do POTA with each other.  Kinda weird for me cuz I'm always alone but it's fun to get out with your buddies to new parks and activate.  Thought this would be a cool video to show others what kinds of things people are doing to get out and portable.
